steps.title: Identify the gap between prior belief and new finding
steps.content: Researchers previously believed anti-bat ultrasonic clicking was limited to a few specific moth groups. Barber et al. then tested 252 genera across most large-bodied moth families and found 52 genera producing these clicks — including geometer genera not previously documented with this defense. The study's key contribution is revealing this ability in far more groups than expected.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice against the study's finding
steps.content:
• Choice A — unlike the 52 moth genera that emit ultrasonic clicks, most moth genera have likely not developed defenses specifically against bat attacks.: reverses the logic; the passage does not claim most genera lack defenses, and 52 is a larger number than previously known, not a small exception. ✗
  • Choice B — some genera of large-bodied moths may use ultrasonic signaling for purposes other than avoiding capture by predators such as Brandt's myotis.: introduces alternate uses of ultrasound not discussed in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — ultrasound production is only one of a diverse range of effective strategies moths employ to evade bat attacks.: introduces other defensive strategies not mentioned in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — anti-bat ultrasound production may be a more prevalent defense strategy among large-bodied moths than previously known to researchers.: directly reflects the finding that 52 genera possess this defense when researchers had expected only a few. ✓
That matches Choice D — anti-bat ultrasound production may be a more prevalent defense strategy among large-bodied moths than previously known to researchers..
